name: session-metrics
description: Parses Claude Code JSONL logs to produce per-turn token/cost/cache breakdowns. Multi-format export (text/JSON/CSV/MD/HTML) with 5-hour session blocks, weekly roll-up, session duration & burn rate, hour-of-day + weekday punchcard, subagent → parent-prompt token attribution, pluggable chart libraries (Highcharts/uPlot/Chart.js/none), and 9-category turn waste classification (productive/retry/cache/reasoning/file-reread/verbose/dead-end/subagent — stacked bar + drill-down cards in dashboard). HTML timeline includes idle-gap dividers, model-switch dividers, truncated-response badges, cache-break badges, Turn Character column, and per-turn drawer with data-driven classification explanation. v1.27.0 adds a self-cost meta-metric ([self-cost] stderr + HTML KPI card + JSON top-level key, suppressible via --no-self-cost) and a sibling audit-session-metrics skill (Haiku-pinned for ~10× cost savings). v1.29.0 adds a positive_findings array + idle_gap_cache_decay trigger; the schema bumps to 1.1 (additive) and forbids the 'other' enum that was driving Haiku padding antipatterns. v1.30.0 adds a session_archetype classifier (detect-only) + first_turn_cost_share baseline fields; schema bumps to 1.2 (additive). v1.30.1 always exports JSON alongside any --output format so the audit suggestion is always shown. --compare-run spawns two headless claude -p sessions for a one-command A/B benchmark; --compare renders IFEval pass-rate + ratio heatmap from existing JSONLs. Zero network by default, stdlib-only Python.
